perturbation.original_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Protected Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' introduces ambiguity. While the definition remains the same in this section, the rest of the document uses 'Confidential Information.' This change creates a conflict and uncertainty as to whether the terms are interchangeable or have different meanings. The rest of the contract still reffers to 'Confidential Information,' and now this paragraph is reffering to 'Protected Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that any breach of any provisions hereof by Recipient will cause irreparable harm and damage to LSUK . Money damages may not be an adequate remedy and in such event, LSUK , may in addition to other equitable and legal relief that may be available, seek the entry of injunctive relief by a court of competent jurisdiction. In the event a legal dispute arises concerning this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall recover all court costs, expenses, and reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' creates a strong in-text contradiction. The perturbed explanation states that the rest of the document refers to 'Confidential Information.' The contradicted text makes use of the term 'LSUK,' and mentions the 'Agreement', so the document as a whole is indeed being referred to. This means that the change introduces ambiguity and uncertainty as to whether 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information' are interchangeable or distinct, thus contradicting the rest of the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Confidential Information to a third party or permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same except those parties deemed necessary by the undersi gned to evaluate a possible business relationship (including agents, advisors, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, consultants, and lenders).
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees not to copy, duplicate, disclose or deliver all or any portion of the Sensitive Data to a third party or permit any other third party to inspect, copy or duplicate the same except those parties deemed necessary by the undersi gned to evaluate a possible business relationship (including agents, advisors, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, consultants, and lenders).
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data' causes uncertainty because 'Sensitive Data' is not defined anywhere in the agreement. The term 'Confidential Information' has a specific definition at the begining of the document, using 'Sensitive Data' in this section, while the rest of the document keeps using 'Confidential Information' raises the question of whether these terms are interchangeable or if 'Sensitive Data' refers to a different subset of information. This creates a contradiction in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data', while 'Confidential Information' is specifically defined in the agreement. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information'. The use of 'Sensitive Data', which is not defined, creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether 'Sensitive Data' falls under the definition of 'Confidential Information' or represents a separate, undefined category. This ambiguity directly contradicts the original intent and creates potential conflict in enforcement as per the Misaligned Terminology definition, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood that the undersigned may disclose Confidential Information only to parties who (i) require such m aterial for the purpose related to this agreement and (ii) are informed by Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree to be bound by the terms hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood that the undersigned may disclose Confidential Details only to parties who (i) require such m aterial for the purpose related to this agreement and (ii) are informed by Recipient of the confidential nature of the Confidential Details and agree to be bound by the terms hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Details,' we introduce uncertainty about the scope of information covered by the NDA. The change raises the question of whether 'Confidential Details' refers to a specific subset of 'Confidential Information' or if it's meant to be interchangeable. Given that the original text defines 'Confidential Information', and 'Confidential Details' is not defined, raises an internal in-text contradiction, affecting the original definition scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Details' introduces a contradiction because 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined in the contradicted text, providing a comprehensive list of what it encompasses. By using 'Confidential Details' without a definition, the changed text creates ambiguity and uncertainty about whether it refers to the same scope as 'Confidential Information' or a subset, thus contradicting the clear definition provided in the contradicted section. The explanation states this creates an internal in-text contradiction, affecting the original definition scope. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient further agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by parties in the preceding sentence, and that these parties will not use any of the Confidential Information for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible business relationship and not in any way detrimental to LSUK .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient further agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by parties in the preceding sentence, and that these parties will not use any of the Protected Data for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible business relationship and not in any way detrimental to LSUK .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', similar to other perturbations, creates confusion as the term 'Protected Data' is not explicitly defined within the agreement, leading to a lack of clarity regarding its scope and coverage, in addition to the fact the the original definition of 'Confidential Information' is being overlooked. This contrasts to the original and defined 'Confidential Information', thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', which is not defined anywhere in the document. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient's responsibilities were initially tied to the defined 'Confidential Information'. Now, the agreement refers to a non-defined 'Protected Data', creating ambiguity and a potential conflict regarding what the Recipient is responsible for, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This shall n ot, however, prevent Recipient from disclosing to others or using in any manner Confidential Information : (i) which has been published and has become part of the public domain and not caused by acts or omissions by the receiving party ; (ii) which has been furnished or made known to the undersigned by third parties as a matter of right without restriction of disclosure, or ; (iii) which the undersigned can show was already in its possession at the time it entered into this Agreement and which was not acquired directly or indirectly from LSUK , it's representatives, or employees.
perturbation.changed_text: This shall n ot, however, prevent Recipient from disclosing to others or using in any manner Confidential Data: (i) which has been published and has become part of the public domain and not caused by acts or omissions by the receiving party ; (ii) which has been furnished or made known to the undersigned by third parties as a matter of right without restriction of disclosure, or ; (iii) which the undersigned can show was already in its possession at the time it entered into this Agreement and which was not acquired directly or indirectly from LSUK , it's representatives, or employees.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of the exceptions. Is 'Confidential Data' a subset of 'Confidential Information', or are they interchangeable? The contract only defines Confidential Information and using Confidential Data here creates an In-Text contradiction, since it's not defined. The contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is defined in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data', which is undefined in the contract. This introduces ambiguity and contradiction, as it is unclear whether the exceptions apply to 'Confidential Data' as well. This creates a conflict, because the contract defined Confidential Information, and now it's using 'Confidential Data' and it's unclear if they are the same, creating a strong in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in perpetually and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all data and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in perpetually and upon request, Recipient will promptly return all information and materials furnished by LSUK , or provide written certification of its destruction and destroy any internal analyses and/or work papers related to these discussions .
perturbation.explanation: By substituting 'data' with 'information', this perturbation introduces ambiguity. While data and information can be similar, the contract previously defined specific types of information as 'Confidential Information'. This alteration implies either a broader requirement to return all information (not just confidential), or creates uncertainty if only 'Confidential Information', as previously defined, needs to be returned. This is a direct in-text contradiction due to the change and the definition provided previously
perturbation.contradicted_text: That all information, data, and materials furnished , either orally or otherwise by LSUK shall be considered "Confidential Information ," which includes all technical and non -technical info rmation concerning the past, present, and future business p ractices and/ or plans to include , without limitation, LSUK 's highly proprietary automated print on demand book manufacturing process , its processes, features, functions, performance, components, s ubsystems, use, technology (whether owned or licensed), intellectual property, resources, research, innovations, products or service offerings, strategic partners, techniques or processes, software, patent applications, inventions, trade secrets, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, strategies, or studies and all tangible and intangible property of any kind, whether conveyed in writing or orally by LSUK or its representatives to Recipient.
